label: Baseline Diagnosis
summary: Patient presented with heavy bleeding caused by Uterine Fibroids, resulting in Anemia (Hb 9.4). Acute bleeding is being managed with hemostatic and hormonal medications.

sections.title: Gynecological Findings
items: Uterine Fibroids: Benign tumors in the uterus. Primary cause of heavy menstrual bleeding.
Nabothian Cysts: Benign mucous cysts on the cervix. Require no intervention.
sections.note: Fibroids are the root cause of the current acute bleeding episode and the resulting anemia.

sections.title: Vitals & Blood Work
sections.note: Low hemoglobin (Anemia) explains the elevated resting heart rate (97 bpm) as the heart pumps faster to supply oxygen.

consultationPlan.note: To review fibroid size via ultrasound and discuss long-term management (medical vs. surgical).
